retry_reason: Failure (e.g., -ENOENT) is typically due to missing device tree entries or hardware configuration, which is not transient; retrying would yield the same error.
contract_summary: This function acquires exclusive reset controls (via devm_reset_control_get_exclusive) for various ath10k AHB hardware sub-blocks (core cold, radio cold, radio warm, radio srif, cpu init) and stores them in the driver's private `ar_ahb` structure. Each call obtains a reference to the reset controller; if any acquisition fails, the function returns an error and the caller must handle it (e.g., abort probe). The reset controls are devm-managed, so they are automatically released when the device is removed — the caller must NOT manually release them. The function does not transfer ownership of the critical variables (reset handles) to the caller via a return value; they are accessible through the driver context. Error paths are clean: previously acquired handles remain valid and will be freed by the devm framework on driver unbind.

check.reason: Each call to devm_reset_control_get_exclusive returns a reset_control pointer with its refcount increased. The returned pointer is stored in the corresponding ar_ahb field and the access path from that field to the refcount (field->refcnt.refcount) matches the callee's internal path (res.refcnt.refcount). The type chain reset_control--kref--refcount_t is consistent for all operations. All candidate operations are correctly extracted.
